subject: Particle picking in 3D from tomogram
body:
Hi,

I am trying to do automatic particle picking from my tomograms. 

My input was a cropped and binned reconstructed tomogram (which was 
reconstructed in imod) and which I changed to .mrc. I didn’t check 
“Preprocessing” “CTF” and “Motion”. I checked “Pick Particles” and used 
“BoxNet2_20180602”. I then went to “Match Template” and inserted a 
low-resolution structure of my particle of interest that I got from 
aligning and averaging a few manually picked particles. I then pressed 
“Start Processing”.

 

The output gave an “average” folder that contained flattened 2D versions of 
my tomogram and template; a “denoising” folder also with flattened 2D 
versions of my tomogram and template; a “mask” folder containing empty 
images. Then there were the “star” files containing x and y coordinates of 
the picked points, which seemed to have been picked from the flattened 
tomogram which contained very little information because it was flat, and 
so the points had no meaning.  


I would like to have particles picked from the 3D tomographic volume. What 
would you recommend?


Thank you,

 

Zipora

 



Here is the exe output documentation:
